item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was marvelous , I honestly shivered at the narration and emotions that this conveyed . The world building and the revelations where truly magnificent and the nod to the wanderer's library at the end of the article was equally magnificent. Simply flawless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, it's one of the universes referenced in Document 5012-JE.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: According to the tags, it's part of the canon!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this somehow connectied to End of Death?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have a few SCP-5012-# Is that the numbered ones put together?

ETA: ah, okay, it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The item itself is remarkably simple, yet entirely dreadful. I also love how much of the lack of information is based around bureaucratic errors and other Foundation-specific incidents.

I agree with others that I'm not sure the final log ties everything together, but I was nevertheless caught up in the sense of having felt something and being unable to express it. So yeah, this got my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
when I read "golgotha memorial orchestra", I went like this: O.o

then I got to the crosslink at "further investigation into the events of January 1st, 1924 has been authorized by Foundation Overwatch" and went: :O (I haven't read that canon yet, but I've had it bookmarked to read for ages).

I think I need to reread the documentation that was written by Goss, a few times. I admit I found it slightly anticlimactic that he was THE 5000-B despite only being part of the anomaly for about two years, but that's a pretty minor nitpick. anyway, I feel like there is something, maybe a lot of things, that I'm not quite catching. that says a lot more about me than the piece. I get confused by anything that isn't explained simply enough for my soon to be 5 year old to understand. :-)

I still really enjoyed the imagery and writing, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: fully agreed with this analysis. it's okay. it's overwhelmingly okay, in fact. i think a novote is appropriate here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm finding myself very much in agreement with this. The ending note is delightfully obtuse, but it doesn't wrap up the story that you're trying to tell here in a satisfying way. Your set up was excellent, with lots of little details for readers to pick up, hopefully to be expanded on later. But then you just… don't?

This one is a novote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was pretty great when I was reading it as a draft, and that doesn't change now. The mystery here is quite thought-provoking, and the imagery that is present in DOCUMENT 5000-JE is incredibly vivid, almost to the point of being palpable.

The fact it also ties in with the On Mount Golgotha canon does the added benefit of giving it a stronger anchor to the rest of the wiki than some of the other articles (mine included), which makes this entry feel more like a lived-in-universe than others.

All in all, an excellent entry, and a solid +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The mystery is the vague sort I love. This is excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the bizarre, slightly sinister, feeling this gives off. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this has by far the strongest imagery of the initial bunch of entries (maybe of the entire contest, but we are only on day one, so who knows?) and is one of my favorites so far. everything is satisfyingly set up and executed, and the last note has very strong 3001 vibes in the best way possible. upvoted with gusto.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, you obviously had me at “Golgotha”, but I enjoyed this regardless - the early going in particular. The anomaly is well set-up and full of threads to pull on.

I was a little less convinced by the final log, as I’m not sure it ties everything together as clearly as I think you intend. I’m also a bit suspicious about some of that text as having been typed, rather than spoken.

There were a couple of typos - a “virbant” and I think some missing full stops. And is it “synaesthesia”?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Somewhat torn between novoting and upvoting this, though not enough that I'm not hitting the +1 anyway. The imagery of the central anomaly and the description of this poor bastard's journey throughout the Spider fiddleverse are both very well-drawn; vivid and striking enough that I'm willing to forgive what are ultimately small, but still… sticky nitpicks I have with some of the ends that the article leaves untied (e.g. some of the gaps in the history of the item.)

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Everyone
Everyone around here
Everyone is so near
It's holding on
It's holding on

Thanks to Uncle Nicolini, Tracque_, Jacob Conwell, A Random Day for their critique!

Preferred runner-up slots:

  • 5012
  • 5995
  • 5200
  • 5225
  • 5775
  • After that, uh, i guess whatever remaining palindrome, then any combination of 5, 7, 0, and/or 2 that ends in 25, 00, 50, or 75.

neighborhood4
crownoflove
everpink
